title: China-to-Senegal Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: ASN and UEMOA / ECOWAS Regional Standards Context
rows.target_requirement: Senegal belongs to the West African UEMOA / ECOWAS regulatory and standards context, and ASN states that it represents Senegal in regional and international standardization bodies, including ECOWAS/ECOSHAM, ARSO/ORAN, AFSEC, ISO, and the IEC Affiliate Country Programme. Senegal is not a CEMAC member state, so CEMAC norms should not be treated as Senegal market-access law unless a Senegal buyer, donor program, cross-border project, or contract expressly incorporates them. For Senegal solar inverter files, prioritize ASN, UEMOA / ECOWAS, AFSEC, IEC, and project documents over generic CEMAC claims.
target_standards: ASN national standards and standards-information functions
UEMOA / WAEMU references where adopted or required
ECOWAS / ECOSHAM references where adopted or required
AFSEC electrotechnical standardization references where adopted or required
Non-Senegal CEMAC references only where expressly incorporated by contract or project specification
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese exporters may have regional Africa tender experience or CEMAC-related documents from Central African projects. Those documents can be useful background, but they should not be presented as Senegal proof unless the Senegal file expressly asks for them.
china_standards: China-origin tender and regional Africa reference files
rows.gap: The regional-standards gap is jurisdictional. Confirm whether the Senegal project is asking for ASN, ECOWAS, AFSEC, IEC, donor-bank, or contractual CEMAC language, and do not overstate CEMAC as a Senegal national requirement.

mandatory_status.target: ASN or adopted Senegal, UEMOA, or ECOWAS requirements may be mandatory where referenced by law, tender, or contract; CEMAC references are not a default Senegal mandatory route
mandatory_status.cn: China regional-reference documents do not create Senegal legal compliance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Senegal as UEMOA / ECOWAS, not CEMAC. Use CEMAC language only if the Senegal project file expressly incorporates it; otherwise build the standards file around ASN, IEC, UEMOA / ECOWAS, AFSEC, and customer requirements.

rows.requirement: ASN Standards Route and IEC 62109 Safety Evidence
rows.target_requirement: ASN is Senegal's national standards body and provides access to Senegalese, regional, and international standards information. For PV inverters, IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 safety evidence is a practical benchmark when a Senegal project, buyer, public tender, insurer, or conformity route asks for internationally recognized inverter safety testing. ASN's solar-equipment quitus page cites Order 010158 MEPC/DMC of 28 May 2020 and treats eligible solar-equipment quality checks as part of the VAT-exemption route; the requested Decree 2025-1960 market-entry quality-control claim was not confirmed in the public sources reviewed.
target_standards: ASN Senegalese and international standards catalogue
Order 010158 MEPC/DMC of 28 May 2020 for solar-equipment VAT-exemption quality checks
Decree 2025-1960 market-entry quality control - not confirmed in reviewed public sources
Project, tender, insurer, or purchaser technical specifications where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin inverter files often include GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, CQC or CGC certificates, and IEC CB reports. These can support a Senegal technical file, but Chinese voluntary certificates or China-only reports do not automatically prove ASN acceptance or satisfy a Senegal project specification that names IEC 62109.
china_standards: CQC or CGC voluntary inverter certificates
IEC CB Scheme reports where available
rows.gap: Prepare a clause crosswalk from the China file set to IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2, identify any national deviations requested by the buyer or project, and confirm whether ASN, the tender authority, or the customer requires a specific certificate format.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the ASN solar-equipment quitus / VAT-exemption route applies under Order 010158; otherwise usually voluntary or project-driven unless a Senegal regulation, tender, procurement file, insurer, or contract makes the named standard mandatory
mandatory_status.cn: Usually voluntary or project-driven for inverter safety unless required by a Chinese buyer, grid project, or certification scheme

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109 evidence is the right safety language for Senegal-facing inverter files, but it should be tied to the actual ASN, tender, customer, or project requirement before calling it mandatory.

rows.requirement: China GB/T Baseline vs Senegal Market Entry Package
rows.target_requirement: A Senegal-facing inverter file should combine the requested ASN or international standards evidence, IEC 62109 safety documentation where specified, CRSE project-route evidence for generation or export, and Senelec or concession-operator connection acceptance for grid-tied systems. These gates are cumulative when they apply.
target_standards: ASN standards or project-specified international standards
IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 where required
CRSE electricity-sector authorization or classification
Senelec or concession-operator grid connection acceptance
rows.china_equivalent: A typical China file set may include GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964, NB/T 32004, IEC CB reports, CQC or CGC certificates, Chinese EMC reports, and Chinese labels/manuals. This is a useful starting point but not a Senegal approval package.
china_standards: GB/T 37408 for photovoltaic grid-connected inverter technical requirements
GB/T 19964-2024 for photovoltaic power-station grid-connection technical requirements
rows.gap: Build a China-to-Senegal crosswalk, translate key technical materials into the language required by the project, identify China-only assumptions, and obtain Senegal project-owner, regulator, or grid-operator acceptance before relying on the China baseline.

mandatory_status.target: Mixed and project-specific - regulatory and grid approvals are mandatory where in scope; ASN or IEC evidence may be mandatory only where law, tender, contract, insurer, or buyer requires it
mandatory_status.cn: Mixed - many China inverter documents are voluntary, certification-scheme, or project-driven and do not transfer automatically

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not treat the China GB/T file set as a Senegal approval file. It needs an ASN, IEC, CRSE, and Senelec-facing crosswalk for the actual project route.

source.publisher: Standardization Administration / National Public Service Platform for Standards Information, China

rows.requirement: CRSE Regulatory Route for Grid-Connected Solar PV
rows.target_requirement: CRSE describes Senegal's electricity sector as structured around Senelec, independent power producers, self-producers, and rural electrification operators. A PV inverter is not cleared for a grid-connected generating project merely because the product has a China certificate or an IEC report. The project owner must confirm the applicable CRSE route for generation, self-production, surplus sale, tariff treatment, concession area, and connection authority.
target_standards: CRSE electricity-sector regulatory framework
Senegal electricity-sector authorizations for producers, self-producers, IPPs, and rural electrification operators
Project-specific power purchase, surplus-sale, tariff, concession, or connection documentation
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese domestic grid acceptance records may show technical suitability for a China project, but they do not establish Senegal generation authorization, tariff eligibility, or Senelec connection acceptance.
rows.gap: Separate the product file from the project authorization file. Define whether the Senegal system is self-consumption, surplus export, IPP, mini-grid, rural electrification, or utility-scale, then align inverter protection, metering, and commissioning evidence to that route.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where Senegal electricity-sector authorization, tariff approval, concession rules, or grid export applies; may differ for off-grid or purely behind-the-meter systems
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese project approval does not replace CRSE or Senegal project authorization

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A Senegal grid-tied solar project needs a Senegal regulatory path, not only an inverter certificate. Confirm CRSE classification before treating the equipment as project-ready.

source.publisher: Commission de Régulation du Secteur de l'Energie (CRSE), Textes Réglementaires

rows.requirement: Senegal 50 Hz Grid Settings and Protection Windows
rows.target_requirement: Senegal grid projects should be configured for the local nominal 50 Hz AC supply and the voltage, frequency, trip, reconnect, and ride-through windows required by the connection authority or project specification. Nominal 50 Hz compatibility is only the starting point; commissioning evidence should show the actual Senegal grid profile loaded into the inverter.
target_standards: Senegal project or connection-authority grid-profile settings
Senelec or concession-area protection and commissioning requirements
Inverter protection and anti-islanding settings for nominal 50 Hz operation
rows.china_equivalent: China also uses nominal 50 Hz AC grids, so the headline frequency is not the main difference. The practical gap is the allowed voltage and frequency windows, reconnection timing, ride-through profile, anti-islanding settings, and installer access controls accepted for the Senegal site.
rows.gap: Lock the Senegal firmware profile, document all protection thresholds, and ensure installers cannot accidentally commission China-market default settings at a Senegal connection point.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as a connection and commissioning requirement where Senelec, a concession operator, CRSE route, project owner, or installer acceptance specifies frequency and protection settings
mandatory_status.cn: Nominal 50 Hz is common, but Chinese protection profiles are not automatically valid for Senegal

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] 50 Hz compatibility is necessary but not sufficient. Senegal-facing inverter files should show the exact grid profile and protection settings accepted for the connection point.

source.publisher: Commission de Régulation du Secteur de l'Energie (CRSE), Senegal

rows.target_requirement: CRSE identifies Senelec as the historical operator for production, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity. For many grid-tied PV systems, the connection package must satisfy the relevant Senelec or concession-area technical review, including single-line diagrams, inverter protection settings, anti-islanding evidence, metering arrangement, commissioning procedure, and any export or surplus-sale controls.
target_standards: Senelec or concession-area connection review
CRSE electricity-sector operator framework
Site-specific interconnection agreement, metering arrangement, and commissioning acceptance
Anti-islanding, protection, and export-control requirements specified for the connection point
rows.china_equivalent: China grid reports may document anti-islanding and protection functions, but their default voltage, frequency, reconnection, ramp-rate, and export-control settings may not match the Senegal connection point. Senegal-specific parameter sheets and commissioning records should be created.
rows.gap: The connection gap is site-specific acceptance. Provide Senegal-facing wiring diagrams, protection-threshold tables, anti-islanding test reports, firmware profile names, meter-interface drawings, and installer commissioning records.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection, export, or surplus-sale acceptance where Senelec or another concession operator controls the connection point
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid approval is not accepted as Senegal connection approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not ship a grid-tied Senegal project with only a generic China grid certificate. Build a connection pack for Senelec or the actual concession operator.

source.publisher: Commission de Régulation du Secteur de l'Energie (CRSE), Senegal
